title: Fragrant Dense Malaysian Rice Sushi
description: A quick, herb‑infused Malaysian rice sushi that uses Chinese five‑spice powder and is shaped into nigiri‑style pieces. This dense, FODMAP‑friendly dish cooks in a single cazuela pot.
instructions: Rinse the rice until the water runs clear and place it in a cazuela with one cup of water.
Add the coconut milk, Chinese five‑spice powder, bruised lemongrass, pandan leaf, minced ginger, salt, and lime zest to the pot and bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed.
Transfer the cooked rice to a bowl, remove the lemongrass, pandan leaf, and ginger, and fold in shredded chicken, tamari, sesame seeds, and scallion tops.
Let the mixture cool slightly, then shape individual nigiri‑style pieces by hand or with a small mold.
Serve the dense rice sushi at room temperature or warmed gently in the cazuela until ready to eat.
